evidence_basis_summary: Humans set starting templates and topic areas, but idea filtering (Semantic Scholar novelty check) and paper acceptance (automated reviewer) are delegated to the system in both v1 and v2.
notes: Full text confirms humans scope the research direction, but delegation of filtering is explicit; role pressured but observable.

evidence_basis_summary: Papers note LLM bias in over-estimating novelty/interestingness/feasibility and discuss limitations, but no explicit human-in-loop mechanism check is described in the pipeline.
notes: Upgraded from low: boundary-case evidence shows the role is attenuated rather than absent.

evidence_basis_summary: Both papers concern ML experiment automation (diffusion, LMs, grokking); no social-science construct or perspective disagreement is at stake.
notes: Upgraded to high: source content provides enough detail to confidently mark not applicable for this boundary case.

evidence_basis_summary: Evidence base is ML benchmark datasets (e.g., Shakespeare transformer, Hugging Face Hub); no pluralist-corpus stakes visible in either paper.
notes: Upgraded to high: full-text confirms not applicable.

evidence_basis_summary: Humans design templates, experiment stages (preliminary/hyperparam/agenda/ablation), stopping criteria, agentic tree-search protocol, VLM-feedback loop, and prompt scaffolding across both versions.
notes: Upgraded from medium: protocol/architecture design is the single most-documented human contribution, supporting robust status.

evidence_basis_summary: Papers confine claims to ML experimentation with internal metrics; no cross-population or transportability adjudication is invoked.
notes: Upgraded to high based on full-text scope.

evidence_basis_summary: Fully code-and-benchmark automation with no fieldwork, interview, or interpretive setting in either paper.
notes: Upgraded to high: clearly not applicable.

evidence_basis_summary: v2 explicitly discusses submitting AI-generated manuscripts to ICLR workshop peer review, ethics/safety considerations, authorship framing, and open-sourcing - authors foreground who is answerable.
notes: Upgraded from medium: v2 abstract and section 4 directly concern accountability (peer-review disclosure, ethical/safety discussion).

evidence_basis_summary: No codebook/label construct work at issue in ML-experiment automation.
No subgroup disagreement or representational pluralism concern in these ML automation papers.
No cross-context transport pressure in the automated ML pipeline described.

Paper discusses auto-reviewer calibration against ICLR 2022 OpenReview ground truth (class-imbalance thresholding, @6 cutoff), but this is an internal AI-reviewer ablation rather than social-science simulator calibration.
notes: Upgraded from low: calibration language is present but applied to a different register; keeping not_applicable per codebook scope.

evidence_basis_summary: No interviewer or fieldworker role in an automated ML pipeline.
No tacit/archival interpretive work required; all artifacts are code, logs, and numerical outputs.
Idea filtering via Semantic Scholar API and automated reviewer triage (accept/reject threshold) replace human first-pass screening; near-human accuracy claimed on ICLR 2022 data.
notes: Upgraded from low/not_applicable: full text shows this is a shrinking cluster, not absent - changed observability to yes.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: Aider executes plan-directed coding, experiment iteration, and debugging with ground-truth validation metrics; tree-search nodes automate routine coding against benchmark scores.
notes: Upgraded from low/not_applicable: routine coding/iteration is explicitly delegated; changed observability to yes.

evidence_basis_summary: Both papers describe AI drafting manuscripts section-by-section (intro, methods, results, related work), LaTeX compilation, citation insertion via Semantic Scholar, and VLM-driven figure/caption refinement.
notes: Upgraded from low/not_applicable: manuscript drafting is the flagship shrinking task; changed observability to yes.
